description: Standard: adapt dry, ironic voice to new topic

input.voice_sample: There is a particular kind of meeting that exists in every organization I have ever observed — one that nobody calls, but that everyone attends. It has no agenda. Its purpose, if pressed, would be described as 'keeping everyone aligned,' which is the professional world's most expensive euphemism for not trusting anyone to do their job without supervision.
input.new_topic: Write a passage in this voice about corporate wellness programs and why they solve the wrong problem.

input.voice_sample: The numbers don't lie. They just omit.
input.new_topic: Write one sentence in this voice about opinion polls.

input.voice_sample: Brussels has a talent for producing documents of extraordinary length about subjects of extraordinary narrowness. The 340-page technical annex to the Digital Markets Act is, in this respect, a masterwork — a monument to the conviction that if you regulate something precisely enough, it will behave. European regulation, at its best, is like a cathedral: centuries in the making, awe-inspiring in its ambition, and somewhat impractical for daily life.
input.new_topic: Write a passage in this voice about the EU's attempt to create a common charger standard for all electronic devices.
